creator: Alexander Terp
description:
RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-14 (rad v0.10.1): no recursion depth limit; deep self-recursion now hits a raw Go `fatal error: stack overflow` (exit code 2) rather than a clean rad error. (Originally reported as "hangs forever"; current behavior is a Go-level crash.)

**Repro:**
fn infinite():
    return infinite()

infinite()   // -> fatal error: stack overflow (Go runtime, ~1GB stack)

Most languages impose a recursion limit (e.g. Python's ~1000) and raise a clean error.

**Fix:** add a call-depth counter in the interpreter and emit a clean rad error past a limit. Pairs with the circular-data crash (both are unbounded recursion crashing Go).
title: Infinite recursion has no depth limit - must be killed externally
